description.en: A traditional pulse in Sri Lanka's Dry Zone, tolerant of drought and poor soils; often intercropped in rain-fed uplands. Research in 1995–1996 evaluated 8 extra-short-duration and 2 short-duration pigeon pea genotypes at two April/May sowing dates, with and without supplementary irrigation — rain-fed grain yields ranged 0.07–0.47 t/ha in late sowings, rising to 1.7 t/ha with irrigation. Internationally, pigeon pea is grouped into early (120–140 days), medium (160–180 days), and long (>180 days) maturity classes; most Sri Lankan cropping systems use early or short-duration genotypes to fit the dry-zone rain window.

duration_days.quote: medium (160-180 days) and long (>180 days) maturity groups, with a small portion belonging to the early (120-140 days) maturity group

expected_yield_kg_per_acre.quote: Grain yields of the pigeonpea genotypes under rainfed conditions ranged from 0.07 to 0.47 t ha⁻¹ in the late sowings, while irrigated yields reached 1.7 t ha⁻¹ in the late sowings
expected_yield_kg_per_acre.review_notes: 0.07–1.7 t/ha → 28–688 kg/acre — wide range reflects rain-fed vs. irrigated contrast.
